msgs.text:
I read the prompt and prior-turn file as external proposals. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, and verifier exponents modulo M=85667662080.The prior file’s viable thread is the Turn 9–14 “dynamic tame-inertia” candidate; the earlier candidates were explicitly refuted by finite-tame or index-two subgroup tests.

The surviving candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2 and η=2π on prime-to-2 tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1.

The constants are π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η=−2π ≡50932630783(modM).I do not see a new finite-tame counterexample to this candidate. The Turn 9–14 computations, if correct, establish much more than numerical evidence: the finite tame pro-2 kernels have the expected Frattini module, nondegenerate mod-2 cup product, and dyadic orientation behavior. This matches the general Jannsen–Wingberg strategy described in Neukirch’s report: the known local presentations are obtained by analyzing Demuškin groups over finite tame subextensions and their tame action data.Numdam The general finite-presentability context for local absolute Galois groups is also consistent with Jarden–Shusterman’s account.Mathematics at TAU The new issue I found is that the remaining “formation uniqueness” gap is not merely formal. The data listed in Turns 12–14 do not determine the 2-primary Frobenius action, even modulo the third Zassenhaus layer.First, a correction to the trivial tame-inertia block: the prior turns sometimes display the quotient P(T) in the wrong direction. In the trivial τ-block one has(1+T−s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

For even f, s is odd, so 1+T∣1+T−s. Thus the correct elimination is

Y=Q(T)X+Yˉ,Q(T)=1+T1+T−s,not its reciprocal. With this correction the prior unit proof still works, because (1+ζ)(1+Q(ζ)+Q(ζ−1))=ζ−s+ζs+1,so a zero would force ζ2s+1=1, and 2s+1 is coprime to f. Thus the cup-product nondegeneracy result survives, but the corrected quotient matters for any final written proof.Now for the new obstruction-to-proof. Look only at the unramified quadratic quotient. Let U be the pro-2 kernel above it, and let α denote the action of the quotient Frobenius of order 2. The Turn 13 Frattini-module statement gives

V:=U/U2≅F2[C2]⊕F22.

Choose a basisa0,a1,b,c with α(a0)=a1, α(a1)=a0, and α(b)=b,α(c)=c. The quadratic Demuškin initial relation may be writtenq=a0[2]+a1[2]+[b,c].LetL2=U2/U3.

For a free pro-2 group on V, degree 2 is V[2]⊕∧2 V; imposing the one Demuškin initial relation givesL2≅(V[2]⊕∧2 V)/⟨q⟩.

As an F2[C2]-module, writing R=F2[C2] for the two-dimensional regular indecomposable and T=F2 for the trivial module,

V≅R⊕T2.

A direct decomposition givesV[2]≅R⊕T2,and∧2 V≅R2⊕T2.

Before imposing q, degree 2 is R3⊕T4. Since q spans a trivial summand, one obtainsL2≅R3⊕T3.

Degree-one IA automorphisms of U/U3 are controlled by

M=HomF2(V,L2).

Using R∨≅R, T∨≅T, R⊗R≅R2, R⊗T≅R, and T⊗T≅T,

M≅(R⊕T2)⊗(R3⊕T3)≅R15⊕T6.

First-order deformations of the C2 -Frobenius action with the same action on V and the same square α2=Inn(S), modulo IA conjugacy, are measured byH1(C2,M).

Since H1(C2,R)=0 and H1(C2,T)≅F2,H1(C2,M)≅F26.So there is a six-dimensional space of nonconjugate first-order Frobenius-action deformations already modulo U3. These deformations keep the same H1 -module, the same Demuškin quadratic form, the same Bockstein/sign character, and the same square condition modulo U3.One explicit class is easy to see. Define a degree-one IA derivation d:V→L2by d(b)=b[2],d(a0)=d(a1)=d(c)=0.

The element b[2]is fixed by C2 and is not in the image of 1+α on L2, so this d represents a nonzero cohomology class. The deformed actionαd=(1+d)α has the same action on V, preserves the quadratic relation through degree 2, and satisfies the same square condition modulo U3, but it is not IA-conjugate to α at this level.This does not refute the Turn 9–14 candidate. It refutes a possible shortcut proof: the finite-tame kernel being the correct oriented Demuškin group is not enough to force the full profinite extension over the tame quotient. The final proof must identify the actual IA class of the 2-primary Frobenius action, not merely the abstract Demuškin type of each kernel.

The natural next invariant is the C2 -equivariant action on U/U3, or dually the action on the appropriate mod-4 Kummer layer. The prior orientation checks identify the Bockstein class inside each kernel, but they do not yet identify how Frobenius acts on the mod-4 lift of H1. That is exactly where the six-dimensional ambiguity above lives.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 9–14 candidate remains the strongest surviving presentation. The trivial τ-block elimination should use Q=(1+T−s)/(1+T); with that correction, the prior cup-product nonsingularity proof still works. New this turn: in the unramified quadratic case, the listed Demuškin, cup-product, Bockstein, and square data leave a nonzero deformation spaceH1(C2,Hom(V,L2))≅F26 for the 2-primary Frobenius action modulo U3.

GAP: The candidate is not refuted, but the remaining formation/rigidity step cannot be proved from the Turn 12–14 invariants alone. One must compute and match the actual Frobenius IA class, equivalently the equivariant mod-4 Kummer action or the action on U/U3.

NEXT: Compute the candidate’s explicit C2 -action on U/U3 in the unramified quadratic case, express its class in the six-dimensional space above, and compare it with the arithmetic Frobenius action on L×/(L×)4 for the unramified quadratic extension L/Q2.[BREAKTHROUGH]
